# Brown Butter Chocolate Chip Cookies
NOTE: This is an AI-generated test document, it's not real, and any resemblance to real people or places is coincidence.

Adapted from the recipe on the back of the Nestle Toll House bag, but browning the
butter first because that's what won over the neighborhood cookie swap two years running.

## Ingredients
- 1 cup unsalted butter
- 1 cup brown sugar, packed
- 0.5 cup granulated sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2.5 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 2 cups semisweet chocolate chips
- 1 cup chopped walnuts (optional)

## Instructions
1. Brown the butter in a saucepan over medium heat until it smells nutty and turns
   amber, about 6-8 minutes. Cool slightly.
2. Whisk browned butter with both sugars until glossy.
3. Beat in eggs one at a time, then vanilla.
4. Fold in flour, baking soda, and salt until just combined.
5. Stir in chocolate chips and walnuts.
6. Chill dough at least 1 hour, ideally overnight.
7. Scoop onto parchment-lined sheets, bake at 375°F for 10-12 minutes.
8. Cool on the sheet for 5 minutes before moving to a rack.

## Notes
Chilling overnight makes a noticeably chewier cookie. This is the batch Aunt Carol
requests every time she visits, always asking to skip the walnuts.
